title: plantago tomentosa subsp. paralias
intro: Plantago tomentosa subsp. paralias, also known as woolly plantain, is a perennial plant species in the plantain genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as aucubin and allantoin. The health benefits of Plantago tomentosa subsp. paralia include wound healing, reduction of inflammation, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Plantago tomentosa subsp. paralia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
